metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 1,959 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +30% more 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Combustion (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,157 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +12% more physical survivability: try Spectral Schematics over Spirit Of Amir (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,358 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 3% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 32% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 30% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Blood Well” ⇒ sustain; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 50.4% over 88,100 games, magnitude not simulated.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,157 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,358 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 3% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 18% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 16% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 18% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 18% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 16% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 18% of your damage.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 17% of its bite; you give up 15% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

laneSteer.provenance: “Sanguine Slash” ⇒ ability-burst; the sim models this augment — field jungle 47.5% over 61,631 games.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
